title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Automation and Data Client Systems · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for ssrf, dns rebinding, injection, response validation, and least authority, producing defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers defense-in-depth outbound request policy after a delayed changed case. Learners pro…
objectives: Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Ssrf Destination Policy · changed case · Review Ssrf Destination Policy

review case — compliance handoff automation receives https://127.0.0.1:8005/meta, a decimal IP alias, a hostname that rebinds, and a redirect to file://.
